policy: Expanded snippets do not promote candidates. They only strengthen display and audit of rows already in authoritative_scope_rows.

records.snippet_en: ollowing the issuance of this conclusion. Cordially, Edwin R. Vargas S. Assurance AssociatePartner Ernst & Young Audit S.A.S. EDWIN RENÉ VARGAS SALGADO Digitally signed by EDWIN RENÉ VARGAS SALGADO Date: 2025.03.27 14:45:08 -05'00' A member firm of Ernst & Young Global Limited Free Translation ANNEX 1 Subject Matter The sustainability information identified in the indicators included in the Report printed and included by Ecopetrol S.A. on its website1 it is presented in the following table: Content/Indicator Information Insured Material topic: Materials and waste DJSI 2.3.2 DJSI Waste Disposal Total waste recycled or reused: 192,689 MT - Waste disposed: 344,370 MT - Waste disposed in landfills: 212,033 MT - Waste incinerated with energy recovery: 828 MT - Waste incinerated without energy recovery: 581 MT - Other types of disposals: 130,929 MT - Waste disposed of by unknown disposal method: 0 MT Scope: Businesses where Ecopetrol S.A. has operational control (including Cartagena refinery). DJSI 2.3.3 DJSI Hazardous Waste Total hazardous waste recycled or reused: 13,020 MT Total hazardous waste recycled or disposed: 74,673 MT - Waste deposited in security cell: 4,354 MT - Hazardous waste incinerated with energy recovery: 548 MT - Hazardous waste incinerated without energy recovery: 578 MT - Hazardous waste disposed of in other ways: 69,193 MT - Hazardous waste disposed of by an unknown disposal method: 0 MT Scope: Businesses where Ecopetrol S.A. has operational control (including Cartagena refinery). Material topic: Climate change DJSI 2.5.1 DJSI Direct Greenhouse Gas Emissions (Scope 1) Gross direct Scope 1 GHG emissions: 11,817,213 tCO2e Biogenic Scope 1 emissions: 821 tCO2e Scope: Covers the estimation of 100% of the emissions generated in Colombia by the facilities where the Company has operational control, meaning the Upstream assets operated by Ecopetrol and those in the Refining and Industrial Processes Vice Presidency, which includes the two (2) refineries (Cartagena refinery and Barrancabermeja refinery). DJSI 2.5.2 DJSI Direct Greenhouse Gas Emissions (Scope 2) Gross location-based energy indirect Scope 2 GHG emissions: 704,342 tCO2e Gross market-based energy indirect Scope 2 GHG emissions: 859,892 tCO2e Scope: Covers the estimation of 100% of the emissions generated in Colombia by the facilities where the Company has operational control, meaning the

As part of this verification, the Company made adjustments to its methodology to improve accuracy, consistency, and transparency. (DJSI 2.5.1) (DJSI 2.5.2) (DJSI 2.5.3) (CCE-4) (GRI 305-1) (GRI 305-2) (GRI 305-3) (GRI 11.1.5) (GRI 11.1.6) (GRI 11.1.7) (TCFD M-1.2) (SASBEM-EP-110a.1) (WEF 7) Table 8.3. GHG emissions by Scope160 2021 2022 2023 2024 Unit tCO2e Direct GHG Emissions (Scope 1) 12,515,133 12,338,759 12,458,520 11,817,213 Indirect GHG Emissions (Scope 2) Location-based 298,047 301,947 305,395 704,342 Market-based 583,362 500,772 468,921 859,892 Indirect GHG Emissions (Scope 3) 138,535,802 148,218,864 152,963,989 151,117,258 Gases Included in the Calculation CO2, CH4, N2O 160. The figures presented in the 2023 Integrated Management Report for the 2021-2023 period encompass an estimate of 100% of the emissions generated in Colombia by the facilities under the Company's operational control. This includes the Upstream assets operated by Ecopetrol and the Refining and Industrial Processes Vice Presidency, which covers the two refineries: Cartagena and Barrancabermeja. GHG emissions by Scope160 2021 2022 2023 2024 Unit tCO2e Direct GHG Emissions (Scope 1) 12,515,133 12,338,759 12,458,520 11,817,213 Indirect GHG Emissions (Scope 2) Location-based 298,047 301,947 305,395 704,342 Market-based 583,362 500,772 468,921 859,892 Indirect GHG Emissions (Scope 3) 138,535,802 148,218,864 152,963,989 151,117,258 Gases Included in the Calculation CO2, CH4, N2O 160. The figures presented in the 2023 Integrated Management Report for the 2021-2023 period encompass an estimate of 100% of the emissions generated in Colombia by the facilities under the Company's operational control. This includes the Upstream assets operated by Ecopetrol and the Refining and Industrial Processes Vice Presidency, which covers the two refineries: Cartagena and Barrancabermeja.
